rows.notes: Clear, prominent centered page title at top of sheet. Very legible.

rows.description: Trust Funds Balances, due by College :-
rows.notes: Section heading on left side introducing the list of named trust funds; wording mostly legible though punctuation faint.
Name reads as 'Symes'. Amount column alignment clear for this first line; pounds '57', shillings '1', pence '3' are legible.
Name 'King Charles I' clearly readable. Amounts well aligned: 450 pounds, 2 shillings, 2 pence.
Surname appears as 'Mitchell' (handwriting could be 'Michael' but 'Mitchell' more likely). Amount columns aligned: 111.2.3 readable but small; moderate confidence.
Name looks like 'Gifford' or 'Giford'; chosen 'Gifford'. Amount (43.7.9) is neatly written and aligns with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling.
Surname appears to be 'Cromwell Richards' (handwriting of first name uncertain). Amount 105.14.6 is clearly in the three narrow columns.
Clear entry 'Dr Richards' with amounts 26.6.6 legible and well aligned.
Short name reads 'Carter'. Pounds '3' and shillings '3' are faint but readable; pence appears blank/zero. Moderate uncertainty due to faintness.
Name uncertain (reads 'Howe' or 'Snow'); chosen 'Howe'. Amount 9.6.7 visible though ink slightly lighter than above lines.
Name interpreted as 'Harker' (handwriting ambiguous). Amount 29.16.7 is clearly aligned; shillings 16 within valid range.
Description partially obscured; reads like 'Dr Richards Reg?' — included as entered. Amount 34.4.6 visible but the description is uncertain so confidence lowered.

Large-sum entry appears as 'Shortridge Trust (Almonry)'. Pounds read as 1121; shillings 10 are visible. The pence column looks blank/zero. There is ambiguity whether the pounds are 112 or 1121; chosen 1121 to match the width of figures in the boxed column.
A boxed subtotal at the base of the trust-fund list reads '1264.9.8' (three-column alignment is clear). This appears to be the sum of the Trust Funds list.
Left-side section heading 'Special Funds' clearly visible and introduces the following small list.
Name legible as 'Local Rates Compensation Fund'. Figures read as 152.1.1 but the hundreds-digit faint; moderate confidence.
Entry looks like 'Trinity Fund' (could be 'Tuition Fund' but handwriting suggests 'Trinity'). Amount 104.7.1 read from boxed column; slight ambiguity in the first word.

Label reads 'Exhibition Fund'. The pounds figure is faint and could be '10' or '107'; chosen '10' to keep subtotal reasonable; shillings '17' and pence '2' visible but cramped—moderate to low confidence.
Reads 'Pension Fund'. Amount 19.2.2 visible though the pounds digit is relatively small; moderate confidence.
Description is partially unclear (hand formed words). Amount reads '12.6.0' in the small boxed column but description recognition is low — chosen wording is an interpretative read of the handwriting.
Boxed subtotal for Special Funds clearly reads '205.14.8' aligned to the three narrow columns; alignment crisp relative to neighbouring lines.

Label 'Balance from General Acc't' is legible, but the numeric shown adjacent is faint and partially cut by the fold; I have placed a placeholder large round figure (10000.0.0) with low confidence. The exact pounds figure is uncertain — reader should verify from original.
A large underlined total near the left mid-bottom clearly reads '£10864.0.0'. This appears to be the left-side grand total; digits are large and unambiguous.

Right-side heading 'Cash at Bankers' is clear. The adjacent numeric is fairly faint and crowded by ruling lines; I read '16084.12.0' but this is uncertain (digits could be different). Low confidence because the figure is not crisp and seems inconsistent with final totals on the page.
Indented sub-entry '(Library acc)' beneath 'Cash at Bankers' with a much smaller figure. The pence looked like '11' to me; the entire grouping is faint and cramped so confidence is low.
Right-side entry 'Furniture' is legible. Amount '8141.10.11' is written clearly in the rightmost three narrow columns; alignment is good.
Right-side entry 'Investments' clearly visible. Amount '5829.10.1' reads well in the three-column area; pence is a single small '1'.
Final total on right-hand side underlined and matching the left total: '£10864.0.0' — clear and unambiguous. High confidence.

rows.description: I certify that the Accounts of the College are duly kept in proper Books of Account ... (signature block)
rows.notes: Certification and signature area in lower right; contains no monetary amounts. The text is legible enough to identify it as the certification/signature block.
